hero.title: Lynx Pardelle
hero.subtitle: Pressure, texture, and aftermath for late-night listeners.
hero.description: A tense, immersive catalog where distorted edges, melodic restraint, and visual identity move as one system.
hero.supportNote: Start with one track, turn the volume up, and let the rest of the catalog settle in.

featuresSection.title: Why the sound lands
featuresSection.subtitle: Four pressure points define the project: tension, detail, contrast, and a visual language that refuses to separate sound from image.

features.title: Tactile production
features.description: Layers feel cut, scraped, and placed with intention instead of washed together.
benefits: Hard edges with room to breathe

features.title: Night-driven atmosphere
features.description: The catalog stays tense and immersive without collapsing into shapeless darkness.
benefits: Cinematic weight instead of empty gloom
A mood that holds from start to finish

features.title: Controlled contrast
features.description: Rock force and electronic architecture push against each other instead of blending into compromise.
benefits: Clean tension between impact and space

features.title: Visual system thinking
features.description: Artwork, tone, and interface behave like part of the same release-world.
benefits: Sound and image reinforcing each other

processSection.title: How to enter the world
processSection.sidebarTitle: You do not need to hear everything at once. Move through it in a deliberate order.

processCards: 01 Start on Spotify for releases, follows, and the fastest line back to every new drop.
02 Move to Tidal when you want more space, more depth, and a cleaner view of the production decisions.
03 Use the wider platform spread when you want the project woven into your everyday listening flow.
04 Keep the footer channels nearby for updates, visuals, and direct lines back into the project.

servicesSection.title: Three ways in
servicesSection.subtitle: Different entry points for different listening habits.

services.title: First exposure
services.description: The direct route for new listeners who want the atmosphere quickly.
benefits: Save the artist on your main platform
Come back at night, not in passing

services.title: Deep immersion
services.description: For listeners who care about texture, space, and what happens beneath the obvious layer.
benefits: Use headphones or a focused speaker setup
Stay long enough for the details to surface

services.title: Repeat contact
services.description: The project opens further on repeated listens instead of immediate over-explanation.
benefits: Track the visual and sonic continuity
Build your own route through the catalog

listeningModes.title: Late-night headphones
listeningModes.description: The cleanest way to catch the tension between detail and impact.

listeningModes.title: Cinematic focus
listeningModes.description: Work, writing, or visual sessions that need atmosphere without softness.

listeningModes.title: Transit and motion
listeningModes.description: Best when movement and repetition sharpen the pulse instead of diluting it.
benefits: Walking and night driving both fit
Leaves a stronger afterimage after the track ends

faqSection.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqSection.subtitle: Everything you need to start listening with context.

faq.title: What kind of music is this?
faq.content: Experimental electronic rock built around tension, atmosphere, and sharp production detail.

faq.title: Where should I start?
faq.content: Start with Spotify if you want the fastest route into the catalog, then move to Tidal for more depth and fidelity.

faq.title: Is it only on Spotify and Tidal?
faq.content: No. Those are the main entry points, but the project is also present on other major streaming platforms.

faq.title: Why does the visual side matter so much?
faq.content: Because the project is built as one release-world where sound, texture, and image reinforce each other.

faq.title: How do I stay close to new releases?
faq.content: Follow on Spotify or Tidal first, then use the footer channels for visuals, updates, and direct contact points.

finalCtaSection.title: Enter the Lynx Pardelle signal
finalCtaSection.subtitle: Follow now and keep the next release, visual rupture, and update within reach.

data.title: Privacy notice
data.intro: Analytics and contact data are used only to improve the experience and respond through the listed channels.
points: Analytics data is retained only as long as it remains operationally useful.
Personal information is not sold to third parties.
You can request clarification about data handling at any time.
